bounded schema shows thresholds should be declared centrally
runtime convergence has scattered inline thresholds and weights
central option contract feeds multiple surfaces
council convergence can consume same profile shape with council-specific semantics
reference thresholds are locked by tests
existing convergence integration tests are parity landing zone
decay and observation gates have behavior coverage in reference
graph novelty evidence extraction should stay graph-owned while thresholds become profile-owned

summary: Author an ADR for a declarative convergence profile contract before migrating code.
why: The current runtime convergence script embeds per-loop weights and thresholds inline, so later mined signals will keep scattering constants without a governed contract.

summary: Define one shared profile shape with loop-specific semantics rather than one universal formula.
why: Council currently has a separate THRESHOLDS object and score weights; a shared profile shape can unify contract fields while preserving council agreement semantics.

summary: Add parity tests that lock current trace thresholds, stop decisions, and scores before moving constants.
why: The reference repo treats threshold math as testable behavior; our ADR should require equivalent parity before extraction.

summary: Profile graph novelty floors and future decay/confirmation knobs, but leave graph signal extraction in coverage graph code.
why: Coverage graph owns signal computation today, while novelty floors are consumed in the CLI; the ADR should split evidence extraction from threshold policy.

summary: S6-03 itself was unworked; prior S2/S3 iterations covered individual score-delta, observation threshold, and decay mechanisms, so this iteration contributes the cross-file ADR synthesis rather than another signal-specific finding.
